title: tectaria heterosora
intro: Tectaria heterosora, also known as the 'Kapok fern', is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Tectaria heterosora include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, with some communities also using it as a natural remedy for wounds and injuries.
